intro: Brachypodium poa, also known as false oat-grass, is a perennial grass native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic activities, providing potential health benefits in reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and enhancing immune function. The bioactive constituents of Brachypodium poa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Brachypodium poa is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be used in combination with other herbs to create infusions and decoctions for various health benefits.
